behaviors.description: Only files changed in the pull request are analyzed — unchanged files are not scanned.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at a module handoff: PR-change list provider -> file-selection/filtering -> analyzer dispatcher. A unit test of just the filter misses wiring bugs (path normalization, rename handling, pagination merge). Real OS/network is not required by this behavior text, and no AI output is involved, so System/Agentic/Workflow are not required.
behaviors.test_description: Wire diff-ingestion module, file selector, and analysis scheduler in-process. Feed a fixture PR diff containing added/modified/renamed files plus many unchanged repo files. Assert only changed paths are dispatched to analysis and unchanged paths are never requested.

behaviors.description: Findings are grouped by severity (error, warning, info) in the posted PR comment.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is in composing outputs across modules: linter findings -> severity grouper -> comment renderer/post payload. Unit-testing formatter alone can miss schema/enum mismatches between modules. Higher levels are disqualified because behavior correctness is deterministic text grouping, not real OS/network timing nor AI quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run aggregator + markdown/comment builder together with mixed-severity findings. Capture the outbound comment payload via an in-process fake comment client and assert findings are grouped under error/warning/info sections with correct counts and membership.

behaviors.description: A pull request that touches 200 files completes analysis within the 10-minute Actions timeout.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly a runtime/timeout SLO tied to GitHub Actions environment. In-process tests can hide real subprocess startup costs, filesystem throughput, and runner resource limits. Therefore REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE push this to System.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the actual lint step (same entrypoint used by GitHub Action) against a generated/fixture PR change set of 200 files on a CI-like runner. Measure wall-clock end-to-end duration (including file discovery, lint subprocesses, and comment generation) and assert completion under 10 minutes.

behaviors.description: When the linter process crashes on a malformed file, the remaining files are still analyzed and results posted.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on real process crash semantics ('linter process crashes'). Mocking as a thrown exception may miss crash/exit-code/stream-handling behavior. Needs subprocess resilience verification plus continued pipeline execution; no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run the pipeline with the real linter executable on a corpus containing one malformed file that triggers a hard crash (non-zero/abnormal exit) and multiple valid files. Assert pipeline records the crash, continues analyzing remaining files, and still posts a final aggregated comment.

behaviors.description: Duplicate findings on the same line from overlapping rules are deduplicated in the output.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is deterministic dedup logic (keying by file/line/rule-equivalence). This can be caught at a single function/object boundary with no I/O. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: it does not require real subprocess/network or AI output, only correct dedup semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Call the dedup function/object with synthetic findings containing intentional same-line overlaps from multiple rules and near-duplicates that should remain distinct. Assert output keeps one canonical entry per true duplicate and preserves non-duplicates.
